---
title: Guía de Incorporación del Equipo - Claro Launcher
project: Claro Launcher - Android TV STB
version: 1.0
date: 2026-02-20
audience: Nuevos Miembros del Equipo
---

# 🚀 Guía de Incorporación - Claro Launcher

¡Bienvenido al proyecto de modernización de Claro Launcher! Esta guía te ayudará a ponerte al día rápidamente.

**Tiempo de Lectura Estimado:** 30-45 minutos  
**Tiempo de Configuración:** Ambiente completo = 1-2 horas

---

## 👋 Welcome Aboard!

You're joining an exciting modernization initiative to upgrade the Claro Launcher Android TV application. This 12-week project focuses on:

- 🔒 **Security:** Harden against OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities
- 📈 **Quality:** Improve code coverage from 55% to 75%
- ⚡ **Performance:** Clean architecture, fast builds, smaller APK
- 👥 **Team:** Modern patterns, better processes, continuous learning

---

## ⏱️ 30-Minute Quick Start

### 1. Clone & Setup (10 min)

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/clarolatam/claro-video-android-tv-stb.git
cd claro-video-android-tv-stb

# Install Android Studio (if needed)
# https://developer.android.com/studio

# Open project
open -a "Android Studio" .

# Wait for Gradle sync to complete
# (File → Sync Now if necessary)
```

### 2. Build & Run (10 min)

```bash
# Build the project
./gradlew clean build

# Expected: BUILD SUCCESSFUL ✅
# Time: ~3-5 minutes (depends on machine)

# Run tests
./gradlew test

# Expected: All tests pass
```

### 3. Review Key Docs (10 min)

Read these **in order:**

1. 📄 [README.md](../README.md) - Framework overview (5 min)
2. 📄 [QUICK_START.md](QUICK_START.md) - Project structure (5 min)
3. 📄 Skim [ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md](ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md) - Reference as needed

**Done!** You can now understand the codebase structure and contribute.

---

## 🛠️ Complete Setup (1-2 hours)

### Prerequisites

**Required:**
- macOS, Linux, or Windows 10+
- 8GB RAM minimum (16GB recommended)
- 20GB disk space
- Java 11+ (JDK)
- Android Studio 2021+

**Optional but Recommended:**
- Git client (SourceTree, GitKraken, or CLI)
- IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate (Android Studio is built on it)
- Docker (for CI/CD simulation)

### Step-by-Step Installation

#### Step 1: Install Java (10 min)

```bash
# Check if Java is installed
java -version

# If not installed:
# macOS with Homebrew:
brew install openjdk@11

# Ubuntu/Debian:
sudo apt-get install openjdk-11-jdk

# Windows:
# Download from https://openjdk.java.net/
```

#### Step 2: Install Android Studio (20 min)

1. Download from https://developer.android.com/studio
2. Follow installation wizard
3. Launch Android Studio
4. Install SDKs:
   - Android SDK 33 (target)
   - Android SDK 29 (minimum)
   - Build Tools 33.0.0+

#### Step 3: Clone Repository (5 min)

```bash
# HTTPS (simpler for first-day)
git clone https://github.com/clarolatam/claro-video-android-tv-stb.git

# SSH (if SSH keys configured)
git clone git@github.com:clarolatam/claro-video-android-tv-stb.git

cd claro-video-android-tv-stb
```

#### Step 4: Configure Local Properties (5 min)

```bash
# Create local.properties file
cp local.properties.example local.properties

# Edit with your paths (automatic for most setups):
# sdk.dir=/Users/[yourname]/Library/Android/sdk
# ndk.dir=/Users/[yourname]/Library/Android/ndk/[version]

# Verify Android SDK path
echo $ANDROID_HOME
# Should print SDK path, if not:
export ANDROID_HOME=~/Library/Android/sdk
```

#### Step 5: Project Setup (15 min)

```bash
# Open in Android Studio
# File → Open → Select project folder

# Wait for Gradle sync
# (Takes 3-5 min on first sync)

# Check for errors
# View → Tool Windows → Gradle
# Should show: ✅ Tasks completed successfully

# Verify build
./gradlew clean assemble
# Expected: BUILD SUCCESSFUL
```

#### Step 6: Run First Build (10 min)

```bash
# Build debug APK
./gradlew assembleDebug

# Expected output:
# Built the following APK(s):
# - build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk

# Install on emulator/device
./gradlew installDebug

# Run tests
./gradlew test

# Expected: All tests pass ✅
```

#### Step 7: Verify Environment (5 min)

```bash
# Run diagnostic
./gradlew clean build --info 2>&1 | grep -E "(BUILD|ERROR|WARNING)" | head -20

# Should show:
# BUILD SUCCESSFUL
# 0 errors
```

---

## 📚 Learning Path (By Role)

### For Backend/API Developers

**Week 1 Focus:**
1. Read [QUICK_START.md](QUICK_START.md) (15 min)
2. Read [CODE_STRUCTURE.md](CODE_STRUCTURE.md) - focus on `AMXNetworkSdk` module (30 min)
3. Review [BEST_PRACTICES.md](BEST_PRACTICES.md) - Network section (20 min)
4. **Task:** Review `/AMXNetworkSdk/src/main/java/com/amx/launcher/network/` code (30 min)

**Week 2-3 Focus:**
- Training Module 2.1: Coroutines Fundamentals (Watch)
- Review existing network implementations
- Understand Retrofit + OkHttp configuration

### For Android Frontend Developers

**Week 1 Focus:**
1. Read [QUICK_START.md](QUICK_START.md) (15 min)
2. Read [ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md](ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md) (70 min) - all sections
3. Review [DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md](DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md) - UI workflows (45 min)
4. Read [BEST_PRACTICES.md](BEST_PRACTICES.md) (50 min)
5. **Task:** Open `/app/src/main/java/com/amx/launcher/presentation/` and trace a feature

**Week 2 Focus:**
- One-on-one with Tech Lead (architecture questions)
- Training Module 1: Architecture & MVVM
- Code review of 1-2 existing PRs

**Week 3 Focus:**
- Training Module 2: Coroutines & Flow
- Start on first P0 task (pair with senior dev)

### For QA/Testing Engineers

**Week 1 Focus:**
1. Read [QUICK_START.md](QUICK_START.md) (15 min)
2. Review [DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md](DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md) - Testing section (45 min)
3. Read [DIAGNOSTIC_QUALITY.md](../02-analisis/DIAGNOSTIC_QUALITY.md) - Current test state (60 min)
4. **Task:** Run test suite locally, understand test structure (30 min)

**Week 2-3 Focus:**
- Setup CI/CD pipeline for local testing
- Training Module 3: Security Hardening
- Familiarize with JaCoCo coverage reporting

### For DevOps/Infrastructure

**Week 1 Focus:**
1. Read [QUICK_START.md](QUICK_START.md) (15 min)
2. Review [FASE_3.3_CI_CD_AUTOMATION.md](../04-ejecucion/FASE_3.3_CI_CD_AUTOMATION.md) (60 min)
3. **Task:** Setup SonarQube instance or access credentials (30 min)

**Week 2 Focus:**
- Configure GitHub Actions CI pipeline
- Setup APK size/build time monitoring
- Configure Dependency Check scanning

---

## 🎯 Your First Week (Day-by-Day)

### Monday: Environment Setup
- [ ] Install Android Studio + SDKs (1 hour)
- [ ] Clone repository (10 min)
- [ ] Build project locally (15 min)
- [ ] Run tests locally (10 min)
- [ ] Read QUICK_START.md (15 min)
- [ ] Slack introduction to team (5 min)

**Outcome:** Working local environment ✅

### Tuesday: Architecture Understanding
- [ ] Read ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md (70 min)
- [ ] Review CODE_STRUCTURE.md (30 min)
- [ ] Open IDE, explore /app structure (30 min)
- [ ] Attend architecture overview meeting (1 hour)

**Outcome:** Understand 3-layer architecture ✅

### Wednesday: Codebase Exploration
- [ ] Read DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md (60 min)
- [ ] Read BEST_PRACTICES.md (50 min)
- [ ] Review 2-3 recent PRs in your area (45 min)
- [ ] Pair with team member on code review (1 hour)

**Outcome:** Know coding patterns and standards ✅

### Thursday: Training Session 1
- [ ] Attend formal training (Module 1.1 or 1.2) (90 min)
- [ ] Review training materials (30 min)
- [ ] Work through training exercises (30 min)
- [ ] Q&A with trainer (30 min)

**Outcome:** Learn Clean Architecture / MVVM ✅

### Friday: First Contribution
- [ ] Identify first small task (30 min)
- [ ] Setup local branch (10 min)
- [ ] Implement fix/feature (depends on task)
- [ ] Run local tests (15 min)
- [ ] Create PR with description (20 min)
- [ ] Team feedback (async)

**Outcome:** First PR submitted ✅

---

## 🗣️ Obtén Ayuda

### Preguntas Inmediatas (Slack)

**Canales:**
- `#claro-launcher` - Preguntas generales
- `#claro-arquitectura` - Preguntas de diseño/patrones
- `#claro-seguridad` - Inquietudes de seguridad
- `#claro-qa` - Preguntas sobre testing

**Response Time:** Usually <1 hour during business hours

### Office Hours

**Weekly:**
- **Monday 10-11 AM:** Architecture Q&A (Tech Lead)
- **Wednesday 2-3 PM:** Code review help (Senior Dev)
- **Friday 10-11 AM:** Security Q&A (Security Lead)

**How to Join:** Check your calendar invite or ask in Slack

### One-on-One Meetings

**Schedule:**
- Week 1: 30-min with your direct lead (onboarding)
- Week 2-4: Bi-weekly sync with Tech Lead (mentoring)

**Topics:**
- Progress on initial task
- Architecture / pattern questions
- Career development

---

## ✅ Onboarding Checklist

### Hardware & Software
- [ ] Android Studio installed & working
- [ ] Java 11+ installed
- [ ] Repository cloned locally
- [ ] Project builds successfully
- [ ] Tests run locally (all pass)
- [ ] Git configured (name, email)
- [ ] SSH keys added to GitHub (if using SSH)

### Access & Accounts
- [ ] GitHub account access (team repo)
- [ ] Slack workspace access
- [ ] Jira access (development tracking)
- [ ] SonarQube access (code quality)
- [ ] Firebase Console access (if applicable)
- [ ] VPN configured (if required)

### Documentation
- [ ] Read QUICK_START.md
- [ ] Read ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md
- [ ] Read BEST_PRACTICES.md
- [ ] Bookmarked all essential docs
- [ ] Understand 3-layer architecture
- [ ] Know where to find answers

### Team Connection
- [ ] Met Tech Lead (1:1)
- [ ] Met your direct lead (1:1)
- [ ] Joined Slack channels (4 main channels)
- [ ] Added to calendar (office hours, training)
- [ ] Know how to reach people for help
- [ ] Attended team standup (at least once)

### Technical Readiness
- [ ] First PR submitted (Week 1 Friday) ✅
- [ ] Code review feedback incorporated
- [ ] Local tests passing
- [ ] Can explain project architecture (5 min)
- [ ] Know next 1-2 tasks to work on

### Training
- [ ] Training Module 1.1 attended (or scheduled)
- [ ] Training Module assignment confirmed
- [ ] Quiz dates on calendar
- [ ] Handbook reviewed

---

## 🎓 Training Schedule

**All team members required to complete:**

### **Week 1-2: Module 1 (Architecture)**
- **1.1 - Overview:** Feb 28, 1-2 PM (45 min)
- **1.2 - MVVM Deep Dive:** Mar 2, 1-2 PM (60 min)
- **1.3 - Hilt DI:** Mar 4, 2-3 PM (45 min)

### **Week 3-4: Module 2 (Coroutines)**
- **2.1 - Fundamentals:** Mar 7, 10-11:30 AM (90 min)
- **2.2 - Flow & Reactive:** Mar 9, 10-11 AM (60 min)

### **Week 4: Module 3 (Security)**
- **3.1 - OWASP Top 10:** Mar 13, 2-3 PM (60 min)
- **3.2 - Secure Coding:** Mar 15, 2-3 PM (60 min)

**Total Time:** 9-12 hours (spread over 4 weeks)

---

## 🚨 Common Pitfalls to Avoid

### ❌ Don't
- Assume you understand architecture without reading guide
- Skip local testing before pushing code
- Commit hardcoded secrets or credentials
- Ignore code review feedback
- Work on multiple features at once
- Block on questions (ask in Slack!)

### ✅ Do
- Ask questions early and often
- Read the documentation
- Run tests locally before push
- Follow code review process
- Focus on one task at a time
- Share blockers in standup

---

## 🎯 30-Day Onboarding Goals

### By End of Week 1
- ✅ Local environment working
- ✅ Architecture understood (basic level)
- ✅ First PR merged
- ✅ Team relationships started

### By End of Week 2
- ✅ Code patterns understood (MVVM, DI)
- ✅ 2-3 PRs merged
- ✅ Training Module 1 completed
- ✅ Can pair with senior dev

### By End of Week 3
- ✅ Contributed to P0 or P1 tasks
- ✅ Training Modules 2-3 in progress
- ✅ Comfortable with code review process
- ✅ Can explain architecture to others

### By End of Week 4
- ✅ Independent contributor (minimal review needed)
- ✅ All training modules completed
- ✅ 5-10 PRs merged
- ✅ Comfortable asking for help when stuck

---

## M01: Primera Semana

### Objetivos Semana 1

```
Semana 1: Foundation & Environment Setup

Dia 1 (Monday):
├─ Welcome & introductions (30 min)
├─ Team sync: Projects & processes (1h)
├─ Setup environment (2-3h)
│  ├─ Clone repo
│  ├─ Install Android Studio
│  ├─ Configure JDK/SDK
│  └─ First build attempt
└─ Review documentation (1h)

Día 2 (Tuesday):
├─ Deep dive: Architecture overview (2h)
│  ├─ Clean architecture review
│  ├─ 3 layers explained
│  └─ Module structure tour
├─ Code walkthrough: Main module (1.5h)
│  ├─ MainActivity
│  ├─ Navigation graph
│  └─ DI setup
└─ Q&A session (30 min)

Día 3 (Wednesday):
├─ Hands-on: First build from scratch (1h)
├─ Running tests locally (1h)
├─ Code review training (1h)
│  ├─ PR review de ejemplo
│  ├─ Leave comments
│  └─ Safe refactoring
└─ Git workflow practice (1h)

Día 4 (Thursday):
├─ Tech stack deep-dive (1.5h)
│  ├─ Kotlin Coroutines
│  ├─ Hilt DI
│  ├─ Room database
│  └─ Retrofit networking
├─ Small task: Fix lint errors (2h)
└─ Review + questions (1h)

Día 5 (Friday):
├─ Week 1 retrospective (30 min)
├─ Week 2 planning (30 min)
├─ 1:1 with tech lead (30 min)
├─ Optional: Learn Gradle basics (1h)
└─ Social: Team chat
```

**Success Criteria Semana 1:**
- ✅ Environment fully setup & builds successfully
- ✅ First PR submitted (lint fixes or docs)
- ✅ Attended all training sessions
- ✅ Comfortable navigating codebase

---

## M02: Segunda Semana

### Objetivos Semana 2

```
Semana 2: First Contribution & Understanding Flow

Lunes (Monday):
├─ Code review training deep-dive (1h)
├─ Testing basics (Junit, Mockito) (1.5h)
├─ Start small task (feature/feature-name) (3h)
│  ├─ Create branch
│  ├─ Make changes
│  └─ Write tests
└─ Questions & blockers (30 min)

Martes (Tuesday):
├─ Debugging techniques (1h)
│  ├─ Breakpoints
│  ├─ Logcat filtering
│  └─ Memory profiling
├─ Continue small task (3h)
├─ Prepare PR (1h)
└─ Submit first PR! 🎉

Miércoles (Wednesday):
├─ Address PR review comments (2h)
├─ Learn caching & optimization (1h)
├─ 1:1 technical mentoring (1h)
└─ Explore second task (1h)

Jueves (Thursday):
├─ Merge first PR 🚀
├─ Security basics training (1h)
├─ Database queries training (1h)
├─ Start second task (2h)
└─ Code review others' PRs (1h)

Viernes (Friday):
├─ Week 2 retrospective (30 min)
├─ Performance profiling intro (1h)
├─ Week 3 planning (30 min)
├─ 1:1 with tech lead (30 min)
└─ Buffer for blockers (1h)
```

**Success Criteria Semana 2:**
- ✅ First PR merged
- ✅ 80%+ test coverage on your code
- ✅ Attended code review training
- ✅ Debugged a bug using debugger
- ✅ Second task in progress

---

## M03: Arquitectura del Proyecto

### Visión General de 3 Capas

```
CLEAN ARCHITECTURE (Claro Launcher)

┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     PRESENTATION LAYER                  │
│  (Activities, Fragments, ViewModels)    │
│  - UI rendering                         │
│  - User interaction handling            │
│  - LiveData/StateFlow management        │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ├─ Depends on Domain
                 └─ Uses DI (Hilt)
                 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     DOMAIN LAYER                        │
│  (Entities, UseCase, Repositories)      │
│  - Business logic                       │
│  - Pure Kotlin (no Android)             │
│  - 100% testeable                       │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ├─ No dependencies ↓
                 └─ Independent
                 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     DATA LAYER                          │
│  (API, DAO, DataSource, Repository)     │
│  - Network calls (Retrofit)             │
│  - Database (Room)                      │
│  - Local cache                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 7 Modulos Principales

```
1. app/ - Application main
   ├─ MainActivity (Single Activity)
   ├─ Navigation Graph
   ├─ All 3 layers
   └─ DI modules

2. AMXNetworkSdk/ - Networking centralized
   ├─ Retrofit APIs
   ├─ Interceptors
   ├─ DTOs
   └─ Remote data sources

3. AMXEpg/ - Electronic Program Guide
   ├─ Programs + Channels
   ├─ Room database
   ├─ EPG domain logic
   └─ Schedule management

4. AMXPlayer/ - Video playback
   ├─ ExoPlayer integration
   ├─ Stream URLs
   ├─ Playback state
   └─ Quality selection

5. AMXTvInputService/ - Android TV integration
   ├─ Native TV support
   ├─ Remote control handling
   └─ TvProvider

6. LicensedMulticastSolution/ - Multicast streaming
   ├─ License verification
   ├─ Multicast protocols
   └─ AAR library

7. benchmark/ - Performance testing
   ├─ Baseline profiles
   ├─ Benchmarks
   └─ JMH tests
```

---

## M04: Stack Tecnologico

### Core Technologies

| Aspecto | Technology | Version | Usage |
|---------|-----------|---------|-------|
| **Language** | Kotlin | 1.8.22 | Primary (95%) |
| **Framework** | Android SDK | 34 (Pie+) | Target |
| **Build** | Gradle | 8.1.1 | Build system |
| **JVM** | Java | 17 LTS | Compilation |
| **Async** | Coroutines | 1.7.1 | Async tasks |
| **DI** | Hilt | 2.47 | Dependency injection |
| **Network** | Retrofit | 2.9.0 | HTTP client |
| **HTTP** | OkHttp | 4.11.0 | HTTP layer |
| **Database** | Room | 2.5.2 | SQLite ORM |
| **Media** | ExoPlayer | 2.16.1 | Video player |
| **Analytics** | Firebase | 21.5.0 | Crash reporting |
| **Logging** | Timber | 5.0.1 | Logging |
| **Testing** | JUnit 4 | 4.13.2 | Unit tests |
| **Mocking** | Mockito | 5.5.0 | Test mocks |
| **UI Tests** | Espresso | 3.5.1 | UI testing |

### Important Tool Versions

```
Must Know:
├─ Android Studio 2024.1.1 (IDE)
├─ Gradle 8.1.1 (build system)
├─ Java 17 (compilation target)
├─ Kotlin 1.8.22 (language)
├─ Git 2.40+ (version control)
└─ GitHub (code hosting)

Optional but Important:
├─ Docker (CI/CD simulation)
├─ SonarQube (code analysis)
├─ Firebase Console (monitoring)
└─ Google Play Console (distribution)
```

---

## M05: Domain Knowledge

### Key Concepts to Understand

```
1. CLEAN ARCHITECTURE
   ├─ Why: Testability, scalability, independence
   ├─ How: 3 layers with explicit dependencies
   └─ When: All new code must follow this

2. MVVM PATTERN
   ├─ Model = Domain entities + UseCase
   ├─ View = Fragment/Activity
   └─ ViewModel = StateFlow + business logic coordination

3. REPOSITORY PATTERN
   ├─ Abstraction over data sources
   ├─ Fallback: Network → Local cache
   └─ Responsibility: Data consistency

4. DEPENDENCY INJECTION (Hilt)
   ├─ Automatic constructor injection
   ├─ @HiltAndroidApp, @AndroidEntryPoint
   └─ Modules provide singletons + scoped instances

5. COROUTINES
   ├─ Suspending functions (suspend keyword)
   ├─ Lifecycle aware (viewModelScope)
   └─ Error handling (try-catch or CoroutineExceptionHandler)

6. FLOW & STATEFLOW
   ├─ Cold streams (Flow) vs hot (StateFlow)
   ├─ Reactive updates to UI
   └─ Collector pattern in Fragments
```

### Business Domain Knowledge

```
Claro Launcher Context:
├─ TV STB application (not phone/tablet)
├─ Live TV streaming (multicast + HTTP)
├─ Electronic Program Guide (EPG) schedule
├─ Video quality adaptation
├─ User preferences & favorites
├─ Remote control navigation
└─ Crash-free performance critical

Key Pain Points (why this modernization):
├─ High crash rate (94% → target 98%+)
├─ Slow startup (3s → target 2s)
├─ Dead code (old MVP pattern)
├─ Test coverage low (55% → target 75%+)
├─ Security issues (OWASP Top 10)
└─ New requirements (Android TV API 34)
```

---

## M06: Tools y Workflows

### Essential Tools

```
Daily Tools:
├─ Android Studio (IDE)
├─ Terminal / Command line
├─ Git (version control)
├─ GitHub (code review)
└─ Slack (communication)

Development Tools:
├─ Gradle (build system)
├─ Logcat (debug logs)
├─ Profiler (performance)
├─ Layout Inspector (UI debug)
└─ Database Inspector (Room)

Testing Tools:
├─ JUnit 4 (unit tests)
├─ Mockito (mocking)
├─ Espresso (UI tests)
├─ Robolectric (fast tests)
└─ JaCoCo (coverage)

Documentation:
├─ GitHub Wiki
├─ Markdown docs (.github/docs/)
├─ Copilot instructions
└─ Inline code comments (KDoc)
```

### Key Workflows You'll Use

```
1. FEATURE DEVELOPMENT
   git checkout -b feature/my-feature
   # Make changes
   ./gradlew test
   git commit -m "feat(...): description"
   git push origin feature/my-feature
   # Open PR on GitHub

2. CODE REVIEW
   # Review others' PRs
   # Leave constructive comments
   # Approve when ready

3. DEBUGGING
   # Set breakpoint in IDE
   # Shift+F9 to debug
   # Step through code
   # Inspect variables

4. TESTING
   # Write tests for your code
   ./gradlew :app:test
   # Aim for 80%+ coverage

5. BUILD
   ./gradlew clean build
   ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug
   # Install on device
```

## M08: Canales de Comunicacion

### Official Channels

```
📱 SLACK CHANNELS (Primary Communication)

#claro-launcher (Main channel)
├─ Announcements
├─ Daily standups
├─ General questions
└─ Celebrations 🎉

#claro-launcher-dev (Development)
├─ Code questions
├─ PR discussions
├─ Build issues
└─ Technical deep-dives

#claro-launcher-design (Design)
├─ UI/UX discussions
├─ Wireframes, mockups
├─ Design reviews
└─ User feedback

#architecture-decisions (ADRs)
├─ Architecture Decision Records
├─ Tech stack discussions
├─ Best practices
└─ Long-term planning

#security-team (Security)
├─ Vulnerability reports
├─ Security practices
├─ Penetration test results
└─ CVE tracking

Private Messages:
├─ Personal questions
├─ One-on-ones
├─ Sensitive feedback
└─ Quick clarifications

GitHub Issues:
├─ Bug reports
├─ Feature requests
├─ Documentation
└─ Public discussions

Email:
├─ Formal approvals
├─ External communication
├─ Official records
└─ Off-hours urgent contact
```

### Response Time SLA

```
Channel           | SLA        | Use for
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~|~~~~~~~~~~~~|~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Slack urgent   | <15 min    | Blockers, critical
Slack normal   | <2 hours   | General questions
GitHub issue   | <24 hours  | Tracked tasks
Slack DM       | <30 min    | Personal
Email          | <1 business day | Formal
```

---

## M09: Meetings y Ceremonias

### Weekly Meeting Schedule

```
MONDAY (Team):
├─ 9:00am - Team Standup (15 min)
│  └─ What each person is doing this week
├─ 10:00am - Architecture Sync (30 min)
│  └─ Design reviews, AR decisions
└─ 1:00pm - Code Review Sync (30 min)
   └─ Discuss complex PRs

TUESDAY (Your Growth):
├─ 2:00pm - 1:1 with Direct Lead (30 min)
│  └─ Your progress, blockers, feedback
└─ 3:00pm - Tech Deep-dive (1h)
   └─ Rotating topic (Coroutines, Testing, etc.)

WEDNESDAY (Tech Sync):
├─ 10:00am - Tech Lead Office Hours (1h)
│  └─ Free-form Q&A
└─ 2:00pm - Testing & QA (30 min)
   └─ Test strategy, coverage goals

THURSDAY (Planning):
├─ 9:30am - Sprint Planning (1h)
│  └─ Next week's tasks, estimation
└─ 2:00pm - Performance Review (30 min)
   └─ Metrics, bottlenecks

FRIDAY (Retro & Social):
├─ 9:00am - Week Retrospective (30 min)
│  └─ What went well, improvements
└─ 4:00pm - Social (optional)
   └─ Team hangout, gaming, etc.
```

### Ceremony Formats

```
STANDUP (15 min):
├─ What I did yesterday
├─ What I'm doing today
├─ Any blockers?

CODE REVIEW (30 min):
├─ Present complex PR
├─ Q&A from reviewers
└─ Decision on approach

PLANNING (1h):
├─ Review backlog
├─ Estimate effort points
├─ Commit to sprint goals

RETROSPECTIVE (30 min):
├─ Went well: ✅
├─ Didn't go well: ❌
└─ Action items: 🎯
```

---

## M10: Escalation Paths

### When Things Go Wrong

```
LEVEL 1 - Unblock Yourself (15 min)
├─ Check documentation
├─ Search Slack history
├─ Ask in public channel
└─ If stuck → LEVEL 2

LEVEL 2 - Ask Your Lead (30 min)
├─ Slack your direct lead
├─ Explain the problem
├─ Show what you've tried
└─ If still stuck → LEVEL 3

LEVEL 3 - Escalate Up (1h)
├─ Tech lead involvement
├─ Pair programming session
├─ Architecture decision
└─ If critical → LEVEL 4

LEVEL 4 - Emergency (Crisis!)
├─ Production is broken
├─ Cannot proceed without fix
├─ Page on-call engineer
└─ All hands on deck
```

### Common Issues Quick Resolution

```
"I can't build!"
├─ Step 1: ./gradlew clean
├─ Step 2: ./gradlew build
├─ Step 3: If fails → paste error in #claro-launcher-dev
└─ Usually resolved in <5 min

"Tests are failing!"
├─ Step 1: Check git diff
├─ Step 2: Run locally: ./gradlew test
├─ Step 3: Review error message carefully
└─ Step 4: If environmental → ask lead

"Code review stuck on comment"
├─ Step 1: Ask for clarification in PR
├─ Step 2: If disagreement → discuss in Slack
├─ Step 3: Escalate for decision if needed

"I don't understand the code"
├─ Step 1: Read inline comments + KDoc
├─ Step 2: Check related files
├─ Step 3: Ask in #claro-launcher-dev
└─ Step 4: Book 1:1 if complex topic
```

---

## M11: Week 1 Tasks

### Monday - Setup & Welcome

```
☐ 1. Welcome email & intro call (30 min)
     └─ Meet tech lead, your lead, team overview

☐ 2. Slack setup (15 min)
     └─ Join all channels, add avatar, set status

☐ 3. Hardware & accounts (1h)
     └─ Laptop, GitHub account, email, Slack

☐ 4. Clone repository (10 min)
     bash:
     git clone https://github.com/clarolatam/claro-video-android-tv-stb.git
     cd claro-video-android-tv-stb

☐ 5. Read quick start (15 min)
     └─ README.md + QUICK_START.md

Expected Status: Environment boots, initial confusion = normal!
```

### Tuesday - Deep Dive Architecture

```
☐ 1. Install Android Studio (1h)
     └─ macOS/Windows/Linux installer, full SDK

☐ 2. Open project & build (1.5h)
     bash:
     ./gradlew clean build
     
     Expected: Builds successfully in 3-5 min

☐ 3. FirstBuild review call (30 min)
     └─ Tech lead walkthrough of build output

☐ 4. Read ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md (1h)
     └─ Focus: M01-M05, skip implementation details

☐ 5. Watch demo video (15 min)
     └─ YouTube: App startup & EPG navigation demo

Expected Status: Understand 3-layer architecture conceptually
```

### Wednesday - Code Navigation

```
☐ 1. Code tour in IDE (1h)
     ├─ Navigate to MainActivity
     ├─ Follow NavigationGraph
     ├─ Inspect ViewModel patterns
     └─ Review DI setup (Hilt modules)

☐ 2. Run app in emulator (30 min)
     bash:
     ./gradlew :app:installDebug
     
     └─ Watch EPG load, navigate channels

☐ 3. Lint exercise (1h)
     └─ Fix 3-5 lint warnings (practice PRs)

☐ 4. Read CODE_STRUCTURE.md (45 min)
     └─ Understand module organization

Expected Status: Can navigate code, ready for small task
```

### Thursday - Testing & First Task

```
☐ 1. Testing intro training (1h)
     ├─ JUnit 4 basics
     ├─ Mockito mocking
     ├─ Test naming conventions
     └─ Coverage goals

☐ 2. Run existing tests (15 min)
     bash:
     ./gradlew :app:test
     
     └─ Understand test output

☐ 3. Start first task (2h)
     ├─ Create feature branch
     ├─ Make small change (code style, docs comment)
     ├─ Write 1-2 test cases
     └─ Verify tests pass

Expected Status: Have created feature/branch with 1st task
```

### Friday - Retrospective & Week 2 Plan

```
☐ 1. 1:1 with tech lead (30 min)
     └─ Ask questions, get feedback, blockers

☐ 2. Week 1 retrospective (30 min)
     ├─ What went well?
     ├─ What was confusing?
     └─ Help to improve onboarding?

☐ 3. Peer review practice (30 min)
     └─ Review 1-2 open PRs from team, ask questions

☐ 4. Week 2 planning (30 min)
     └─ Map out first real contribution task

Expected Status: Ready for first PR submission in Week 2!
```

---

## M12: Week 2 Tasks

### Monday - Continue First Task

```
☐ 1. Morning standup (15 min)
     └─ Share week 1 progress

☐ 2. Writing tests training (1h)
     ├─ AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
     ├─ Test naming conventions
     ├─ Mock vs Real objects
     └─ Coverage targets

☐ 3. Add tests to your branc (2h)
     ├─ Unit tests for business logic
     ├─ Mock external dependencies
     └─ Aim for 80%+ coverage

Expected Status: Task complete with tests
```

### Tuesday - First PR Submission

```
☐ 1. Debug training (1h)
     ├─ Breakpoints in Android Studio
     ├─ Stepping through code
     ├─ Variable inspection
     └─ Debugging tips & tricks

☐ 2. Prepare PR (1h)
     ├─ git add .
     ├─ git commit -m "feat(...): description"
     ├─ git push origin feature/your-task
     ├─ Create PR on GitHub
     └─ Add description, link issues

☐ 3. Submit! (15 min)
     └─ 🎉 First PR live!

Expected Status: First PR under review
```

### Wednesday - PR Feedback

```
☐ 1. Code review received (check Slack)

☐ 2. Address comments (2h)
     ├─ Read each comment carefully
     ├─ Make requested changes
     ├─ Add clarifying comments if needed
     └─ git push (auto-updates PR)

☐ 3. Learn optimization (1h)
     └─ Caching basics, DB query optimization

Expected Status: PR comments addressed, waiting for approval
```

### Thursday - Merge & Next Task

```
☐ 1. Get approval ✅
     └─ May need 2 approvals

☐ 2. Merge PR! 🚀
     └─ GitHub merge button or `git merge`

☐ 3. Start second task (2h)
     ├─ Slightly bigger than first
     ├─ Involves 2 layers (domain + data)
     └─ Have tests from start

Expected Status: 1st PR merged, 2nd task in progress
```

### Friday - Week 2 Retro

```
☐ 1. Week 2 retrospective (30 min)
     ├─ First PR merged! 🎉
     ├─ What went easier?
     ├─ What's still confusing?
     └─ Feedback for improved onboarding

☐ 2. 1:1 with lead (30 min)
     ├─ Discuss PR feedback
     ├─ Plan weeks 3-4 contribution level
     └─ Identify weak spots

Expected Status: Ready for independent task ownership Week 3+
```

---

## M13: Week 3-4 Tasks

### Week 3: Ramping Up

```
Week 3 Goals:
├─ Take on 2-3 medium tasks
├─ Start pair programming
├─ Review others' PRs (at least 5)
├─ Attend all training modules
└─ Begin security workshop

Monday-Thursday:
├─ Complete 2 feature tasks (with tests)
├─ Each task: Full cycle (code → test → PR → merge)
├─ Pair program on 1 complex feature
└─ Review + comment on 5+ PRs

Friday:
├─ Week 3 retrospective
├─ 1:1 feedback
└─ Celebrate 3 PRs merged!
```

### Week 4: Productive Contributor

```
Week 4 Goals:
├─ Own 1 larger feature (3+ files)
├─ Guide 1 newer person (peer mentoring)
├─ Present at team sync (your architecture decision)
├─ Security workshop + action items
└─ Performance profiling basics

Monday-Thursday:
├─ Develop feature across domain + data + presentation
├─ Write comprehensive tests
├─ Mentor new person in 1:1
├─ Contribute to architectural discussions

Friday:
├─ Week 4 retrospective
├─ Graduation decision (ready for 100% independent work?)
└─ Onboarding checklist review
```

---

## M14: Graduation Criteria

### When Are You "Done" Onboarding?

```
✅ TECHNICAL COMPETENCE
  ├─ Can navigate codebase independently
  ├─ Understand 3-layer architecture
  ├─ Write tests (unit + integration)
  ├─ Debug issues using IDE tools
  ├─ Know branch workflow + PR process
  └─ Score: Self-assessment + Tech Lead approval

✅ CODE QUALITY
  ├─ Own code achieves 80%+ test coverage
  ├─ Follow style guide (Kotlin conventions)
  ├─ No hardcoded strings or secrets
  ├─ Public APIs documented (KDoc)
  ├─ Lint clean
  └─ Team code review approval

✅ PROCESS MASTERY
  ├─ Create feature branches correctly
  ├─ Write meaningful commit messages
  ├─ Submit complete PRs (with description)
  ├─ Respond to feedback within SLA
  ├─ Merge without conflicts
  └─ Review others' code constructively

✅ TEAMWORK
  ├─ Comfortable asking questions
  ├─ Actve in Slack channels
  ├─ Attend all ceremonies (standup, retro, planning)
  ├─ Help other team members
  └─ Provide feedback on processes

✅ BUSINESS KNOWLEDGE
  ├─ Understand Claro Launcher context
  ├─ Know key features (TV STB, EPG, streaming)
  ├─ Understand performance goals
  ├─ Aware of security requirements
  └─ Know next quarter's roadmap

✅ INDEPENDENT WORK
  ├─ Take on tasks without daily supervision
  ├─ Complete full cycle (design → code → test → review)
  ├─ Identify and fix own blockers
  ├─ Ask for help when truly needed (not overthinking)
  └─ Estimate time accurately
```

### Graduation Checklist

```
❎ PRs Merged:       ___/5 (minimum)
❎ Tests Written:    ___% coverage (target 80%+)
❎ Code Reviews:     ___/10+ reviews of others' code
❎ Tasks Completed:  ___/3+ assigned tasks
❎ Ceremonies:       Attended __% of meetings
❎ Training Modules: Completed __/8 modules
❎ Pair Sessions:    Conducted __/2 sessions
❎ Mentor Check:     Tech Lead sign-off _______
❎ Self Assessment:  Confidence level (1-5) ___/5
```

### Timeline

```
Week 1:   Foundation & Environment
Week 2:   First Contribution (1 PR merged)
Week 3:   Ramping Up (3-4 PRs merged)
Week 4:   Productive Contributor (Ready for graduation)
Week 5+:  Full Team Velocity
```

---

## M15: Milestones Semanales

### Tracking Your Progress

```
WEEK 1 MILESTONE: Environment Ready ✅
├─ Check: Code builds without errors
├─ Check: Tests run locally
├─ Check: Can navigate main.kt
├─ Check: Team faces known, Slack active
└─ Blocker Resolution: Ask in #claro-launcher-dev

WEEK 2 MILESTONE: First PR Merged 🎉
├─ Check: 1+ PR submitted
├─ Check: Received code review feedback
├─ Check: PR merged successfully
├─ Check: All tests pass
└─ Blocker Resolution: 1:1 with tech lead

WEEK 3 MILESTONE: Productive Contributor ⭐
├─ Check: 3+ PRs merged
├─ Check: Reviewed 5+ others' PRs
├─ Check: Pair programmed on complex feature
├─ Check: Attending all ceremonies
└─ Blocker Resolution: Team discussion

WEEK 4 MILESTONE: Independent Work Ready 🚀
├─ Check: Owning medium feature end-to-end
├─ Check: 4-5 PRs merged this week
├─ Check: Mentoring 1 newer person
├─ Check: Security workshop completed
└─ Blocker Resolution: Self-resolution + minimal help

GRADUATION MILESTONE: Full Team Member 👑
├─ Check: All criteria met
├─ Check: Tech lead approval
├─ Check: Ready for independent task ownership
├─ Check: Contributing above onboarding level
└─ Celebration: Team recognition!
```

---

## M16: Expected Deliverables

### By End of Onboarding

```
CODE DELIVERABLES:
├─ 5-8 PRs merged
├─ 80%+ test coverage on owned code
├─ 0 lint violations in your code
├─ 1-2 bug fixes
├─ 1 medium feature (multi-file)
└─ Total: 100-200 LOC contributed

KNOWLEDGE DELIVERABLES:
├─ Can explain clean architecture
├─ Know module dependency tree
├─ Understand MVVM pattern
├─ Master Git workflow
├─ Know testing best practices
└─ Aware of security & performance

PROCESS DELIVERABLES:
├─ Meaningful commit messages
├─ Complete PR descriptions
├─ Constructive code reviews
├─ Timely feedback responses
└─ Collaboration with team

TEAM DELIVERABLES:
├─ Active Slack participation
├─ Attendance at ceremonies
├─ Helping other team members
├─ Onboarding feedback
└─ Suggestionsfor improvements
```

## M17: Support Available

### Resources You Can Access

```
📚 OFFICIAL DOCUMENTATION
├─ README.md (overview)
├─ QUICK_START.md (setup)
├─ ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md (design patterns)
├─ BEST_PRACTICES.md (code standards)
├─ CODE_STRUCTURE.md (file organization)
├─ DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md (daily workflows)
└─ Tech decision logs (.github/docs/)

📝 INLINE CODE DOCUMENTATION
├─ KDoc comments (/** ... */)
├─ Class-level docs
├─ Function parameter docs
├─ Complex algorithm explanations
└─ TODO comments with context

👥 HUMAN SUPPORT
├─ Tech Lead: Big questions, architecture, mentoring
├─ Direct Lead: Daily blockers, feedback, tasks
├─ Team: Code reviews, peer help, pair programming
├─ Slack: Public questions, community answers
├─ 1:1 sessions: Your choice of topics

🎥 VIDEO TRAINING
├─ App demo walkthrough
├─ Clean architecture overview
├─ Testing practices
├─ Debugging techniques
└─ Available in team drive

🔍 CODE EXAMPLES
├─ Reference implementations
├─ Test templates
├─ Architecture patterns
├─ API integration examples
└─ Error handling patterns
```

---

## M18: Feedback Sessions

### Regular Feedback Cadence

```
DAILY:
├─ Standup (15 min) - Share progress
└─ Slack quick checks - Informal feedback

WEEKLY (1:1 with Direct Lead):
├─ What went well?
├─ What was hard?
├─ Blockers or concerns?
├─ Planning next week?
└─ Personal growth areas?

BI-WEEKLY (Tech Lead Check-in):
├─ Technical progress
├─ Architecture questions
├─ Code quality feedback
├─ Learning paths
└─ Mentoring satisfaction?

MONTHLY (Extended Review):
├─ Overall performance
├─ Career development plans
├─ Team dynamics
├─ Compensation/benefits (HR)
└─ Long-term goals

SPRINT RETROSPECTIVE (Weekly):
├─ Team process improvements
├─ What helped onboarding?
├─ Suggest changes to guide
├─ Celebrate wins
└─ Identify systemic blockers
```

### Giving & Receiving Feedback

```
RECEIVING FEEDBACK:
├─ Listen without defending
├─ Ask clarifying questions
├─ Acknowledge the feedback
├─ Plan action steps
├─ Follow up in next session

GIVING FEEDBACK (to others):
├─ Be specific (not "good job" → "Great test coverage!")
├─ Mention the behavior, not the person
├─ Offer suggestions, not criticism
├─ Timely (don't wait weeks)
└─ Private (not in public channels)
```

---

## M19: Technical Setup

### Day 1 Checklist

```
☐ GitHub account access (should be automatic)
☐ SSH key setup:
  ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your.email@claro.com"
  cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub  # Copy to GitHub settings

☐ Git configuration:
  git config --global user.name "Your Name"
  git config --global user.email "your.email@claro.com"
  git config --global core.editor "vi"  # or nano/code

☐ Android Studio:
  ├─ Install from https://developer.android.com/studio
  ├─ Configure SDK location
  └─ Download Android API 34 + emulator

☐ Command line tools:
  ├─ Java: java -version (should show 17)
  ├─ Gradle: ./gradlew --version (in project root)
  ├─ Git: git --version
  └─ ADB: which adb

☐ First build:
  ./gradlew clean build  # Should complete in 3-5 min

☐ Run tests:
  ./gradlew :app:test  # Should pass all tests

✅ Success = Green checkmarks above!
```

---

## M20: Knowledge Acquisition

### Learning Path (Recommended)

```
WEEK 1 (Foundations):
1. Android fundamentals (Activities, Fragments) - 2h
2. Kotlin basics (if needed) - 2h
3. Clean Architecture overview - 1h
4. Gradle & build system - 1h
└─ Total: 6h

WEEK 2 (Design patterns):
1. MVVM pattern deep-dive - 1.5h
2. Repository pattern - 1h
3. Dependency Injection /Hilt) - 1.5h
4. Coroutines basics - 2h
└─ Total: 6h

WEEK 3 (Practical):
1. Testing (JUnit, Mockito) - 2h
2. Debugging techniques - 1.5h
3. Performance profiling - 1h
4. Security basics - 1.5h
└─ Total: 6h

WEEK 4+ (Specialization):
- Choose: Database, Networking, UI, Performance...
- Deep dives based on your interests
- Advanced topics (architecture decisions, etc.)

CONTINUOUS:
- Code review learning (observe patterns)
- Internal tech talks (Slack recordings)
- Pair programming sessions
- Reading others' code (best way to learn)
```

---

## M21: First Contribution

### How to Submit Your First PR

```
PLANNING:
1. Ask tech lead for small task (docs fix, lint cleanup)
2. Understand acceptance criteria
3. Is there a ticket? Comment "/assign @me"

DEVELOPMENT:
1. Create clean branch:
   git checkout main && git pull
   git checkout -b feature/small-task

2. Make minimal changes
   - One feature per PR
   - Don't refactor unrelated code
   - Keep it small (<5 files if possible)

3. Write tests as you code
   - No test = probably wrong approach
   - Aim for 80%+ coverage

4. Verify locally:
   ./gradlew :app:test  # All tests pass?
   ./gradlew lint       # Lint clean?

SUBMISSION:
1. Commit with meaningful message:
   git commit -m "feat(module): User can do X"

2. Push to remote:
   git push origin feature/small-task

3. Create PR on GitHub:
   - Title: "feat(epg): Add search to EPG"
   - Description:
     "## What
      Allow users to search programs by name
      
      ## Why
      Users requested search functionality
      
      ## How
      Added SearchUseCase + UI
      
      ## Tests
      Added SearchViewModelTest (8 cases, 100% coverage)
      
      Fixes #123"
   - Request 1-2 reviewers

RESPONSE TO FEEDBACK:
1. Read comments carefully
2. Make requested changes
3. Commit: git commit -m "address: PR feedback"
4. Push (PR auto-updates)
5. Re-request review

MERGE:
1. Get 2 approvals (usually)
2. Squash if multiple commits (optional)
3. Click "Merge" on GitHub
4. Delete branch

🎉 CELEBRATE - You're a contributor!
```

---

## M22: Full Productivity

### Becoming Fully Productive

```
SIGNS YOU'RE FULLY PRODUCTIVE:
├─ Can estimate task duration accurately
├─ Rarely ask for help on basic questions
├─ Know where to find answers (docs, code)
├─ Comfortable with async programming
├─ Writing tests naturally
├─ Reviewing code thoughtfully
├─ Contributing ideas in architecture meetings
└─ Mentoring others on patterns

TIMELINE TO FULL PRODUCTIVITY:
Week 1:     0% productivity (all learning)
Week 2:     20% productivity (simple tasks with help)
Week 3:     50% productivity (medium tasks, some help)
Week 4:     75% productivity (independently productive)
Week 5-8:   90% productivity (full velocity, some edge cases)
Week 8+:   100% productivity (expert contributor)

NEXT STEPS AFTER ONBOARDING:
├─ Take on sprint tasks at full velocity
├─ Own features end-to-end
├─ Lead code review discussions
├─ Mentor new team members
├─ Contribute to architecture decisions
├─ Suggest process improvements
└─ Plan next career growth
```

---

## M23: Technical Questions

### Getting Answers Effectively

```
How to Ask Technical Questions:

1. DEFINE THE ISSUE
   ❌ "I don't understand Coroutines"
   ✅ "How do I use viewModelScope.launch with try-catch for error handling?"

2. SHOW WHAT YOU'VE TRIED
   ❌ "It doesn't work"
   ✅ "I tried X, then Y, but got error Z. Here's the stacktrace..."

3. INCLUDE CONTEXT
   ❌ "What's this import for?"
   ✅ "In HomeViewModel, we use Flow<State>. Should we use LiveData instead?"

4. PASTE CODE PROPERLY
   ```kotlin
   // Good: code block with syntax highlighting
   viewModel.state.collect { s ->
      // ...
   }
   ```

5. EXPECT RESPECTFUL QUESTIONS BACK
   - "Did you check the docs for this?"
   - "What's the error message?"
   - "Can you share the stacktrace?"

Questions Go To (Choose Right Channel):

Kotlin/Android basics:
└─ #claro-launcher-dev (public, helpful community)

Claro-specific architecture:
└─ @michael.tech (tech lead DM)

Git/workflow questions:
└─ #claro-launcher (or ask lead)

Debugging/testing practices:
└─ #claro-launcher-dev or pair session

Design/UX questions:
└─ #claro-launcher-design

Security/encryption:
└─ @sarah.security (DM)

Urgent/blockers (5+ min stuck):
└─ Slack your direct lead (mention @name)
```

---

## M24: Process Questions

### Understanding the Process

```
"How do I get a task assigned?"
├─ Option 1: Team planning meeting (sprint planning)
├─ Option 2: Ask your direct lead
├─ Option 3: Pick from /issues with "good first issue" label

"What's the PR review timing?"
├─ Target: <24 hours first review
├─ SLA: <48 hours for approval
├─ Team tries to keep <10 open PRs

"When do we release?"
├─ Every 2 weeks (sprint-based)
├─ Staged rollout: 10% → 25% → 50% → 100%
├─ Monday morning releases (to monitor Friday-weekend issues)

"How do I suggest a process change?"
├─ Bring up in retrospective
├─ Or in #claro-launcher-general
├─ Document the proposal
├─ Team votes on trying it next sprint

"What if I disagree with a decision?"
├─ Discuss respectfully (assume good intent)
├─ Escalate to tech lead if needed
├─ But commit to the decision (no undermining)
├─ Revisit after sprint (when you have more data)

"Who decides the architecture?"
├─ Tech lead (Michael) for major decisions
├─ Team consensus when possible
├─ Engineering manager (if there is one) for conflicts
└─ Document decision in Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
```

---

## M25: Administrative Issues

### Non-Technical Problems

```
TIME TRACKING:
├─ Use timesheets system (you'll be enrolled)
├─ Weekly submission (Fridays by 5pm)
├─ Report actual hours, not rounded numbers
└─ Exception: Salary review with manager

BENEFITS & SETUP:
├─ Ask HR for handbook (benefits, holidays, etc.)
├─ Insurance enrollment (health, dental, vision)
├─ 401k setup (retirement)
├─ Paid time off policy
└─ International payroll (if applicable)

EXPENSES & EQUIPMENT:
├─ Laptop: IT desk setup
├─ Phone: If available (ask manager)
├─ Hardware: Monitor, keyboard, etc.
└─ Software licenses: Ask tech lead (Slack Copilot, etc.)

HOLIDAYS & TIME OFF:
├─ US holidays (automatically off)
├─ Vacation: Discuss with your lead
├─ Sick leave: Notify Slack & send email
├─ Jury duty: Approval from manager
└─ Time off approval process: Check company handbook

MANAGER ESCALATION:
├─ If manager is not assigned (ask!)
├─ Schedule 1:1 meetings
├─ Performance reviews (quarterly oder annually)
├─ Compensation discussions
└─ Career development planning

WHO TO CONTACT (Non-Tech):
├─ HR issues → HR department
├─ Technical equipment → IT desk
├─ Benefits questions → HR
├─ Payroll issues → HR
├─ Manager questions → Your manager or HR
└─ General company → HR help desk
```

---

## M26: Emergency Contacts

### Who to Call When...

```
🚨 PRODUCTION IS DOWN:
├─ Slack: @on-call (page the on-call engineer)
├─ Phone: [On-call number in company handbook]
├─ Escalation: Tech lead → Manager
└─ Don't: Try to fix alone (may make worse)

🐛 FOUND A BUG:
├─ Minor: Create GitHub issue (not urgent)
├─ Moderate: Tell team in #claro-launcher-dev
├─ Critical: Page tech lead (@michael.tech on Slack)
└─ Security: Email security@claro.com (don't post publicly!)

🔓 SECURITY ISSUE:
├─ Don't commit code with secrets
├─ Don't post tokens/passwords anywhere public
├─ Email: security@claro.com or @sarah.security
├─ Reference: CWE, CVE if applicable
└─ Severity: Mark clearly (Critical/High/Medium/Low)

💻 HARDWARE FAILURE:
├─ Laptop/desktop issues → IT desk
├─ Monitor/peripherals → IT desk
├─ Network/WiFi → IT team
└─ Phone issues → IT or carrier

🏢 WORKPLACE ISSUES:
├─ Conflict with colleague → Your manager
├─ Harassment/discrimination → HR (confidential)
├─ Mental health → EAP (Employee Assistance Program)
├─ Work-life balance → Your manager
└─ Company culture → Manager or HR

DAILY CONTACTS:

For Code/Architecture:
├─ Slack: #claro-launcher-dev (public)
├─ Or: @michael.tech (DM for quick Q)
└─ Time: Usually responds within 30 min

For Administrative:
├─ Email: your.manager@claro.com
├─ Or: HR help desk
└─ Time: Within 1 business day

For Urgent (Any):
├─ Phone: [Manager number - will be provided]
├─ Slack: @-mention to page (only if urgent!)
└─ In-person: Your manager's desk

After Hours / Weekend:
├─ Only if production is broken
├─ Slack: Page @on-call
├─ Phone: [On-call number]
└─ Otherwise: Wait until business hours

INTERNATIONAL:
├─ Different timezone? Slack usually async
├─ Use video calls for clarification
├─ Document decisions in Slack/GitHub
└─ Don't wait for sync time if low-urgency
```

---

## 🎉 Welcome to the Team!

You're now part of an exciting modernization journey. We're building a better, more secure, more maintainable codebase together.

**Your success is our success.**

If you have suggestions on how this guide could be better, please let us know!
